title: Transactional Outbox, Inbox, and Sagas · Mastery Check
summary: A release board compares near-miss Go and RabbitMQ decisions for transactional outbox, inbox, and sagas and accepts only bounded ownership, causal evidence, and an explicit live-broker transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a Go outbox relay and saga recovery model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Enumerate database-first, publish-first, timeout, crash, confirm-loss, rollback, and duplicate failure windows for a dual write; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Store domain mutation and immutable outbox message identity, type, payload, destination intent, and availability atomically; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Claim bounded outbox rows, publish with backpressure and confirms, mark completion safely, and recover ambiguous attempts through stable identity; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Dual Write Failure Matrix · changed case · Quiz Dual Write Failure Matrix
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Go messaging team handles case 6990 during Go publisher supervisor losing one state notification. Build deterministic Go evidence to compose domain mutation, outbox record, confirmation, and ambiguous-effect recovery; separate deterministic browser proof from race-enabled and live-broker evidence; then make one state notification stale after recovery generation changes. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Which action demonstrates Dual Write Failure Matrix without dropping earlier contracts?

options.text: For diagnose the hidden failure, run one convenient Saga Compensation happy path, omit ownership, error, changed input, goroutine stop, and full-toolchain checks, then accept plausible output. This does not satisfy Saga Compensation for a Go outbox relay and saga recovery model.
Run the Saga Compensation boundary case for a Go outbox relay and saga recovery model, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.

Accept the Saga Compensation result because visible a Go outbox relay and saga recovery model output is the highest authority. That claim fails the diagnose the hidden failure evidence boundary for Saga Compensation.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Saga Compensation
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Saga Compensation: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Go messaging team handles case 9323 during consumer shutdown drill with one blocked handler goroutine. Build deterministic Go evidence to compose domain mutation, outbox record, confirmation, and ambiguous-effect recovery; name the owner and stop path for every goroutine and notification channel; then make one state notification stale after recovery generation changes. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Design saga state, commands, events, timeouts, idempotent steps, compensations, irreversibility, operator intervention, and audit evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Saga Compensation.
steps.why: Retrieving Saga Compensation through the concrete a Go outbox relay and saga recovery model case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Saga Compensation, keep Saga Compensation connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Design saga state, commands, events, timeouts, idempotent steps, compensations, irreversibility, operator intervention, and audit evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

hints: For RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Saga Compensation, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Saga Compensation precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Saga Compensation, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A quiz Go messaging team handles case 9323 during consumer shutdown drill with one blocked handler goroutine. Build deterministic Go evidence to compose domain mutation, outbox record, confirmation, and ambiguous-effect recovery; name the owner and stop path for every goroutine and notification channel; then make one state notification stale after recovery generation changes. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Design saga state, commands, events, timeouts, idempotent steps, compensations, irreversibility, operator intervention, and audit evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

For RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Saga Compensation, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A compensation always restores the world to its exact state before the original action. In Go, a goroutine that publishes after commit closes the dual-write failure window.
